posts.title: How to Choose a Program Evaluator
posts.excerpt: What a program evaluator does, how internal and external evaluation compare, the six questions to ask before you hire, and the green and red flags that separate a strong fit from an expensive disappointment.

posts.title: What Is Critical Analytics?
posts.excerpt: Critical analytics is data analysis that asks how a metric was built, what it measures, and who benefits, not just what the numbers say. Here is what that changes.

posts.title: Student Retention Strategies That Work
posts.excerpt: Student retention strategies that move persistence: leading indicators, early-warning systems, wraparound support, and honest measurement that shows the gap.

posts.title: Enrollment Forecasting 101
posts.excerpt: Enrollment forecasting explained plainly: what it is, the main methods, the data you need, why to disaggregate, and the pitfalls that sink a projection.

posts.title: Internal vs. External Evaluators: How to Decide
posts.excerpt: Internal vs. external evaluator: the honest trade-offs on cost, independence, capacity, and credibility, plus when to choose each and the hybrid middle path.

posts.title: Outcomes vs. Outputs: Measuring Nonprofit Impact
posts.excerpt: Outcomes vs outputs: outputs count activity, outcomes measure change. Learn the logic-model chain, why funders confuse them, and how to measure impact honestly.

posts.title: What Institutional Research Actually Does
posts.excerpt: Institutional research is the campus function behind reporting, accreditation, and student-success analytics. Here is what IR actually does and why it matters.

posts.title: Building an Early-Warning System for Retention
posts.excerpt: How to build an early-warning system for student retention: choose leading indicators, set thresholds, route every flag to a named owner, and close the loop.

posts.title: Culturally Responsive Evaluation, Explained
posts.excerpt: Culturally responsive evaluation treats context, culture, and stakeholder voice as central to validity, not decoration. Here is what that means and why it matters.

posts.title: How to Write the Evaluation Section of a Grant
posts.excerpt: How to write the evaluation section of a grant proposal: questions tied to a logic model, mixed methods, indicators, timeline, and a real plan to use findings.

posts.title: Qualitative vs. Quantitative: Which Method When
posts.excerpt: Qualitative vs. quantitative research, made practical. What each method answers well, the limits of each, and how to choose the method that fits your decision.

posts.title: What a Program Evaluation Costs
posts.excerpt: What a program evaluation costs and why prices vary so widely. The real cost drivers, how to right-size the work, and how to compare quotes fairly.
